pr.title: Baselib: 56 new 100% matches, 54 partial matches across 14 units
pr.author: malvarezcastillo

summary: Large baselib decompilation/matching pass: 56 new 100% matches and 54 new partial matches across 14 baselib-related units, with zero reported regressions. Overall fuzzy match improved from 65.2% to 66.0%, and matched functions increased from 16437 to 16497. The biggest gains were in particle, psappsrt, hsd_3B27, hsd_3AA7, quatlib, sobjlib, texpdag, hsd_3B2E, bytecode, leak, synth, and axdriver, plus cleanup in sislib and semantic variable naming work in lbcardnew.
what_changed: Added or improved baselib decompilation across 14 units, producing 56 new 100% function matches and 54 new partial matches.
Raised project-wide fuzzy match from 65.2% to 66.0% and matched function count from 16437 to 16497, according to the PR body.
particle.c saw the largest single-unit jump, from 4.6% to 22.6%, with 25 new 100% matches and 25 new partial matches.
hsd_3B27 was introduced or newly tracked as a 100.0% unit with 6 new 100% matches.
quatlib was newly matched to 97.6% with 4 full matches and 2 partial matches.
bytecode, leak, and hsd_3B2E were added or substantially filled in as new baselib units, with bytecode at 99.1%, leak at 97.2%, and hsd_3B2E at 92.2%.
synth improved from 59.4% to 67.1%, axdriver from 88.1% to 96.6%, sobjlib from 1.0% to 31.8%, texpdag from 11.4% to 26.9%, and hsd_3AA7 from 0.1% to 11.7%.
sislib had quality cleanup rather than new counted matches, bringing existing functions HSD_SisLib_803A746C, HSD_SisLib_803A7548, and HSD_SisLib_803A75E0 to 100% and improving other partial matches.

Headers and static headers were updated alongside source files, including particle.h/static.h, axdriver.h/static.h, synth.h/static.h, sobjlib.h, hsd_3AA7.h, hsd_3B2E.h, quatlib.h, bytecode.h, and psappsrt.h.

lbcardnew.c and lbcardnew.h received extensive semantic cleanup, replacing register-derived names such as arg0, arg1, var_r28, temp_r3, and temp_r30 with names like card_result, result, save_data, status_out, callback, error, hsd_error, file_idx, pending_ops, saved_error, probe_result, mount_result, enabled, did_disable, filename, file_entries, entry, open_result, free_result, and hsd_result.
smart_moves: The PR combined new matches with header/static-header updates, which helps stabilize prototypes, local static declarations, and struct usage for future baselib work.

The lbcardnew cleanup demonstrates a practical decomp workflow: even when function names remain address-based, local variable and parameter names can encode behavior such as CARD/HSD error conversion, pending async operations, mount/check/delete/rename results, and callback roles.

The sislib cleanup targeted existing partials and brought named functions to 100%, showing that small quality passes on already-decompiled code can yield match improvements without expanding scope to new units.
The PR reported unit-by-unit before/after percentages and new full/partial match counts, making progress auditable and helping future contributors identify high-yield baselib areas.

Semantic names in lbcardnew preserve matching-sensitive control flow while improving readability, especially around CARD async APIs, OSDisableInterrupts/OSRestoreInterrupts pairing, and HSD card error translation.
The matching pass appears broad but organized by unit, with source/header changes kept near their affected baselib modules.
decomp_lessons: For baselib work, keep source and header updates synchronized; many units in this PR required matching .c, .h, and sometimes .static.h edits.
Partial matches are still useful when they advance large low-match units like particle, hsd_3AA7, sobjlib, and texpdag, but reviewers may note structurally non-ideal C even if it passes for now.

Use semantic local names to document inferred behavior without prematurely inventing public symbol names; examples include card_result, hsd_error, pending_ops, saved_error, file_entries, and callback.

Async CARD/HSD card code often benefits from naming around result conversion and pending operation counts, because matching-sensitive code repeatedly stores an immediate API result, converts it to a game/HSD error, increments x8AC for pending callbacks, and returns 0xB while operations remain outstanding.
Interrupt handling patterns should be represented explicitly: store OSDisableInterrupts return value as enabled, track did_disable when the disable is conditional, and restore only if needed.

Reviewer feedback on HSD_TExpSimplify2 indicates that decompilers should prefer the real grouped condition form when evidence suggests the original was a combined if with && rather than nested or multi-if code.
Existing code elsewhere in the same subsystem can be a guide for expression shape: the reviewer specifically noted that the texpdag logic likely exists in TExp already.
Do not overfit only for matching if it obscures recognizable source structure; follow-up cleanup may be expected even when a reviewer allows the code through.
naming_conventions: Replace generic argN parameters with role names when behavior is clear, e.g. card_result, result, save_data, status_out, callback, filename, file_entries, old_name, and new_name.

Replace register-temporary names with operation-result names tied to called APIs, e.g. probe_result for CARDProbeEx, mount_result for CARDMountAsync, check_result for CARDCheckAsync, format_result for CARDFormatAsync, delete_result for CARDDeleteAsync, rename_result for CARDRenameAsync, open_result for CARDOpen, free_result for CARDFreeBlocks, and hsd_result for HSD card helper calls.
Use error and saved_error for values after conversion through lb_80019BB8 or convert_hsdcard_error, and hsd_error for raw HSD card error inputs.
Use pending_ops for lb_80432A68.x8AC-style outstanding async operation counts.
Use enabled for the return value from OSDisableInterrupts and did_disable when restoration is conditional.
Use singular entry or plural entries for iterating file-entry arrays rather than register-derived pointer names.
For stack-only volatile matching artifacts, names like cached_flag, cached_data, and pad_stack are more descriptive than sp18/sp10/sp14, but the diff still marks this area as suspicious with a TODO.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Broad baselib matching can be advanced by introducing many partial functions while still preserving zero regressions, then reporting before/after percentages per unit.

When async API functions produce repeated control-flow patterns, match stability can be maintained by retaining the pending operation counter, saved error local, and interrupt restore order exactly while improving names.
Conditional interrupt restore patterns may require an explicit boolean/int local such as did_disable to match generated code when OSDisableInterrupts is only called inside a branch.

Some stack-shape artifacts may remain as volatile locals to preserve matching, as seen in lb_8001ACEC/lb_8001AE38 where volatile cached_flag/pad_stack/cached_data locals are retained and commented as possibly fake.

Review feedback suggests that C expression shape matters beyond functional equivalence: grouped conditions using && may better match original source style than mechanically expanded multi-if code in texpdag.
Header and static-header declarations are part of the matching tactic for baselib units, not just cleanup; they support correct prototypes and cross-file references.
review_feedback: PsiLupan commented on src/sysdolphin/baselib/texpdag.c in HSD_TExpSimplify2 that the code was likely just a grouped if statement using &&, not a multi-if construction.
The reviewer said they were fairly certain the exact code exists in TExp and allowed it through while noting it should be fixed later.
No other substantive review feedback is present in the provided slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that clean up HSD_TExpSimplify2 in src/sysdolphin/baselib/texpdag.c to use grouped && conditions.
Search for additional TExp source or matching code that mirrors HSD_TExpSimplify2, as referenced by PsiLupan.
Check subsequent baselib PRs for completion of particle, hsd_3AA7, sobjlib, texpdag, bytecode, leak, hsd_3B2E, and quatlib after this broad matching pass.
Look for follow-up cleanup of volatile stack artifacts in lb_8001ACEC and lb_8001AE38 marked with TODO comments.
Review later lbcardnew changes to see whether address-based function names or struct fields such as lb_80432A68.x8AC, unk_34, x50C, and unk_80 were given stronger names.
